subject: SCIENCE
title: Plant Parts and Jobs
objective: Identify common plant parts and functions while recognizing variation.
introduction: Many flowering plants have roots, stems, leaves, flowers, fruits, and seeds, but not every plant has every part at every stage. You will explore examples, sort cards, practice, and answer five questions.

vocabulary.definition: A key word used in the lesson “Plant Parts and Jobs”.
A key word used in the lesson “Plant Parts and Jobs”.
A key word used in the lesson “Plant Parts and Jobs”.

activities.instruction: Study the idea and listen to the narration.
activities.content: Many flowering plants have roots, stems, leaves, flowers, fruits, and seeds, but not every plant has every part at every stage.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: Text alternative: Many flowering plants have roots, stems, leaves, flowers, fruits, and seeds, but not every plant has every part at every stage.

activities.instruction: Explore each learning token, then read the examples.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: A numbered list of the same examples is available for keyboard and screen-reader use.

activities.instruction: Sort each card into “fits the lesson” or “does not fit.”

feedback.correct: Your groups follow the lesson rule.
feedback.retry: Use the explanation, not color or position.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Tap an item and then tap a labeled category; dragging is optional.

activities.instruction: Which part often anchors a plant?

feedback.retry: Read the lesson rule again. flower and fruit do not fit.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: All choices are visible text and selectable with TalkBack.

activities.instruction: Match each example to the lesson idea.

feedback.retry: Return to the explanation and compare one pair at a time.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: A list-based matching control replaces drag gestures.

activities.instruction: Complete the guided review in order.

feedback.correct: You are ready for the five-question check.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Up and down buttons provide an alternative to dragging.

items.prompt: Which statement best matches “Plant Parts and Jobs”?

options.text: Many flowering plants have roots, stems, leaves, flowers, fruits, and seeds, but not every plant has every part at every stage.

items.explanation: The correct response is “Many flowering plants have roots, stems, leaves, flowers, fruits, and seeds, but not every plant has every part at every stage.” because it follows the lesson explanation: Many flowering plants have roots, stems, leaves, flowers, fruits, and seeds, but not every plant has every part at every stage.

The correct response is “roots absorb water and anchor” because it follows the lesson explanation: Many flowering plants have roots, stems, leaves, flowers, fruits, and seeds, but not every plant has every part at every stage.

items.prompt: Which choice does not follow the lesson idea?
items.explanation: The correct response is “Soil is plant food” because it follows the lesson explanation: Many flowering plants have roots, stems, leaves, flowers, fruits, and seeds, but not every plant has every part at every stage.

items.prompt: Which part often anchors a plant?
items.explanation: The correct response is “roots” because it follows the lesson explanation: Many flowering plants have roots, stems, leaves, flowers, fruits, and seeds, but not every plant has every part at every stage.

items.prompt: Which example should be used in the final review?
items.explanation: The correct response is “leaves often photosynthesize” because it follows the lesson explanation: Many flowering plants have roots, stems, leaves, flowers, fruits, and seeds, but not every plant has every part at every stage.

assetSpecs.prompt: Friendly Grade 3 cartoon storybook concept board for Plant Parts and Jobs; rounded shapes, warm dark-brown outlines, tropical palette, no embedded text, uncluttered small-screen layout. Compose overlays provide labels and interactive targets.
assetSpecs.contentDescription: Five connected learning tokens for exploring examples in Plant Parts and Jobs.

safetyNotes: Use the on-screen activity only; no unsupervised physical experiment is required.
